name: Montañas de Prades - circular from Reus via Coll de La Mussara
name_original: Montañas de Prades - circular desde Reus por el Coll de La Mussara
region_intro: Hiking around Reus offers access to diverse landscapes in the Tarragona province of Catalonia, Spain. The region features varied terrain, including the rugged Prades Mountains with dense forests and deep valleys, and the dramatic limestone formations of the Natural Park of Els Ports. Nearby coastal paths provide sea views along cliffs and coves. This combination of mountainous, forested, and coastal environments creates a range of hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Reus The most popular hiking route is Boca de la Mina y Ermita de Santa Anna — circular por Castellvell del Camp, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain and views of the surrounding countryside near Castellvell del Camp. Another top favourite among local hikers is From Reus to Maspujols via the Mig path — circular via the Boca de la Mina, a moderate 11.1 miles (17.8 km) path. This trail leads through the local countryside, connecting Reus with Maspujols. Local hikers also love the Gaudí's Birthplace – Plaça del Mercadal loop from Reus, a 6.1 miles (9.7 km) trail leading through the city's historic center and surrounding areas, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Reus is defined by diverse landscapes including rugged mountains, dense forests, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Reus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Reus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Reus?
A: There are over 100 hiking routes around Reus, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include easy walks, moderate trails, and a few more challenging options, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Reus?
A: Hiking around Reus offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You can explore the rugged terrain of the &lt;strong&gt;Prades Mountains&lt;/strong&gt; with dense forests and deep valleys, the dramatic limestone formations of the &lt;strong&gt;Natural Park of Els Ports&lt;/strong&gt;, or enjoy spectacular sea views along the &lt;strong&gt;coastal paths (Camí de Ronda)&lt;/strong&gt; near towns like Salou and Cambrils. The region also features golden sandy beaches and lush forests.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking trails in Reus?
A: Yes, Reus offers many easy trails suitable for beginners. Out of over 100 routes, 46 are classified as easy. An example of an easy route within the city is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1312427095/la-fumera-del-gas-reusense-and-the-mercado-central-de-reus-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fumera del Gas Reusense and the Mercado Central de Reus — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 5 km.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Reus?
A: The hiking routes in Reus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the opportunity to discover local countryside, and the accessibility to both mountainous and coastal environments.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Reus?
A: Many of the trails around Reus are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1312384757/boca-de-la-mina-y-ermita-de-santa-anna-circular-por-castellvell-del-camp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca de la Mina y Ermita de Santa Anna — circular por Castellvell del Camp&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and takes about 2 hours 36 minutes to complete.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the trails?
A: Hiking around Reus can lead you to various points of interest. You might encounter historical sites like ancient Roman roads or medieval hermitages. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/597960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama (717 m)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenary Pine of l'Aleixar&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4975838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca de la Mina Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salou Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic coastal views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options around Reus?
A: Yes, the diverse terrain around Reus includes many trails suitable for families. The coastal paths (Camí de Ronda) offer leisurely walks with sea views, and the Natural Park of Els Ports has well-marked trails for various fitness levels, including family-friendly options. Look for routes classified as 'easy' for the most suitable choices.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Reus?
A: Reus and the surrounding Tarragona region offer good hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for exploring both the mountainous areas and the coast. Summers can be hot, especially inland, making coastal walks or early morning/late afternoon hikes more comfortable. Winters are mild, allowing for enjoyable walks.

Q: Can I reach hiking trailheads near Reus using public transport?
A: Reus serves as a good hub, and while some local trails might be accessible by public transport, many routes, especially those deeper into the Prades Mountains or Els Ports, may require a car for convenient access to trailheads. For routes closer to the city or coastal towns, local bus services might be an option.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for more adventurous hikers, the rugged terrain of the Prades Mountains offers challenging routes. Additionally, the GR-92, a long-distance trail traversing the Catalan coast, passes through the Tarragona region, providing an enriching experience for serious hikers. There are 4 routes classified as 'difficult' in the area for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Are there any trails that connect Reus with nearby towns or villages?
A: Yes, some trails connect Reus with surrounding communities, allowing you to experience the local countryside. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1312436614/from-reus-to-maspujols-via-the-mig-path-circular-via-the-boca-de-la-mina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Reus to Maspujols via the Mig path — circular via the Boca de la Mina&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.8 km path that leads through the local countryside.
tips: Another highlight of the province of Tarragona, which is located just north of Reus. The driveway of Vilaplana leads over several bends in the natural park and the upland Muntanyes de Prades. This section is heavily forested, which is why the road only provides a view of the coastal region in a few places.
It's a mountain pass well worth climbing. Once you're at the top, you can take several laps around the plateau enjoying spectacular views.

Remains of the old temple of neoclassical construction built between the 18th and 19th centuries. The visible church is currently located in the same space where the medieval church of La Mussara was located, of which some elements that were used for the construction of the new building are still visible.
Old village on a rocky plateau, the view alone is worth the ascent

Between the two largest mountain ranges of the province of Tarragona, Serra de Montsant and Muntanyes de Prades, is the Coll d'Albarca. As it is one of the highest points of this region, you have a beautiful view of the surrounding landscape from the village of the same name.

The Coll d'Alforja connects the Priorat wine region with the coastal area. The western approach from Priorat is winding and the gradient is steady. When traffic is light, this section promises a beautiful ascent and descent.

waypoints.name: Church of Sant Salvador de la Mussara
